global.title: Authorization and Identity

coreIdeas.contextPhrase: Renown bridges Ethereum wallets to W3C DIDs, letting users authenticate to Powerhouse with the same wallet they use for on-chain governance — no separate credentials needed, no central identity provider required.

The Renown SDK provides the programmatic interface for decentralized identity and authentication flows, abstracting the complexity of DID resolution, credential issuance, and verification into a developer-friendly API.

CLI identity uses did:key with Ethereum key delegation, enabling headless automation and CI/CD pipelines to authenticate to reactors without browser-based wallet flows while maintaining the same DID-based trust model.

Two distinct signing mechanisms serve different trust requirements: header signing proves transport integrity and sender identity, while action-level signing provides per-operation non-repudiation for high-stakes document changes.

The ReactorClient automatically signs all outgoing actions, removing the burden of manual cryptographic operations from application code and ensuring that no unsigned operations accidentally reach the server.

The layered authorization model stacks permissions from drive-level defaults through document-level overrides, allowing broad policies to be set at the organizational level while individual documents can tighten or relax access as needed.

Group-based and operation-level permissions enable precise access control — a contributor group might be able to addLineItem but not approveDocument, reflecting real organizational workflows where different roles have different capabilities.
Domain model for individual contributor identities — the identity primitive in the model ecosystem
Network-level organizational entities — complements builder-profile at the org/network layer
Operational hub configuration model — the legal/operational wrapper identity

global.description: Decentralized identity via Renown, DID-based authentication, cryptographic action signing, and the layered permission model that secures Powerhouse documents.
global.orientation: Security in Powerhouse is built on decentralized identity rather than centralized user databases. The Renown system creates DIDs from Ethereum wallets, giving users self-sovereign identities that work across any reactor without a central authority. Every operation can be cryptographically signed — either at the header level for transport integrity or at the individual action level for non-repudiation — and the ReactorClient handles this signing transparently. On top of identity, a layered authorization model controls who can do what: from broad drive-level permissions down to fine-grained operation-level access, with group support for organizational structures. This MOC maps the complete security story from wallet connection through identity creation to permission enforcement.
